extension.valueMarkdown:
His PCP starts workup: TTE (echocardiogram) shows mild left atrial dilation (4.3 cm), preserved LVEF (58%), no valvular disease. TSH is normal. CBC and metabolic panel are normal. CHA₂DS₂-VASc score is 1 (hypertension). His PCP initiates rate control (metoprolol succinate 50mg daily) and anticoagulation (apixaban 5mg BID), then refers Kevin to an electrophysiologist at the University of Minnesota for evaluation of catheter ablation.

The EP specialist at U of M issues a Permission Ticket to access Kevin's records from his PCP, including the ECG, echocardiogram, labs, and medication history relevant to the consult.

Two sites, one state (MN). Tests Permission Ticket constraints on scopes (specialist needs cardiac-relevant data: ECG, echo, labs, medications — not unrelated history) and organizations (community PCP → academic specialist).

resource.conclusion: Atrial fibrillation with ventricular rate of 92 bpm. Normal axis. No ST-segment changes. No pathologic Q waves. No bundle branch block. Normal QTc interval. No prior ECG on file for comparison.

coding.display: CBC W Auto Differential panel - Blood
Comprehensive metabolic 2000 panel - Serum or Plasma

resource.conclusion: Comprehensive metabolic panel within normal limits.
TSH 2.1 mIU/L — within normal limits. Rules out hyperthyroidism as etiology of atrial fibrillation.

Mild left atrial dilation (4.3 cm, LAVI 32 mL/m2) consistent with atrial fibrillation. Preserved left ventricular systolic function (LVEF 58%). No significant valvular disease. Trace mitral regurgitation is physiologic. Normal right ventricular size and function. Normal estimated RVSP (28 mmHg). No pericardial effusion. No intracardiac thrombus identified by transthoracic imaging.

note.text: Blood pressure remains elevated at 142/88 despite 3 months of lifestyle modification. Escalating to pharmacotherapy with lisinopril 10 mg daily.

Newly identified. LDL 138 mg/dL exceeds desirable range. Total cholesterol 218 mg/dL. 10-year ASCVD risk approximately 5.2% (borderline). Patient prefers dietary management over statin therapy initially.

2023-04-18: LDL 128 mg/dL (borderline elevated, improved from 138). Triglycerides 160 mg/dL (mildly elevated). HDL 50 mg/dL. Total cholesterol 210 mg/dL. Patient continues to prefer dietary management over statin. 10-year ASCVD risk reviewed. Will continue annual reassessment.
Clinical diagnosis made by telephone based on symptom duration greater than 10 days with purulent nasal discharge and facial pain/pressure. No red flag symptoms.
Fully resolved with amoxicillin course. No residual symptoms confirmed at annual wellness exam on 2023-04-18. Condition marked resolved.

Discovered on examination (irregularly irregular pulse) and confirmed by in-office 12-lead ECG. Ventricular rate 92 bpm. Symptoms of palpitations and exertional dyspnea over 6-8 weeks consistent with atrial fibrillation. Patient hemodynamically stable. Workup initiated with TTE, TSH, CBC, CMP.

Workup complete: ECG confirms AF rate 92, TTE shows preserved EF 58% with mild LA dilation 4.3 cm, no significant valvular disease, labs normal (TSH 2.1, CBC unremarkable, CMP normal), no reversible cause identified. CHA2DS2-VASc score 1 (hypertension). Treatment initiated: metoprolol succinate 50 mg daily for rate control, apixaban 5 mg BID for stroke prevention. Referred to cardiac electrophysiology for catheter ablation evaluation.

code.text: Diagnosed on transthoracic echocardiogram: LA diameter 4.3 cm, LAVI 32 mL/m2

note.text: Mild left atrial dilation identified on transthoracic echocardiogram. LA diameter 4.3 cm (normal <4.0 cm in males), LAVI 32 mL/m2 (mildly elevated). Consistent with atrial fibrillation-related remodeling.

Patient reports gastrointestinal upset (stomach discomfort and nausea) when taking ibuprofen. Avoids NSAIDs as a result. No true allergic reaction (no rash, urticaria, or anaphylaxis). Intolerance, not allergy.

resource.description: In-office 12-lead ECG tracing showing atrial fibrillation with ventricular rate 92 bpm, normal axis, no ST-segment changes, no pathologic Q waves, no bundle branch block, normal QTc interval.

note.text: 57-year-old male with new-onset atrial fibrillation, mild LA dilation (4.3 cm), preserved EF (58%), CHA2DS2-VASc 1, started on metoprolol and apixaban. Please evaluate for catheter ablation candidacy. Referral includes patient demographics, active problem list, current medication list, ECG report, echocardiogram report, and recent lab results.
